# Questions in Livestream 221

Live Stream #221: The Concise Fun Notes Edition

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=488" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:08</a> | Given Akkadian and Amorite, can Abraham's lines in Genesis 12:11-13 be roughly reconstructed or pronounced? | Not yet; he says the Amorite evidence is still too small, especially for alphabetic Amorite. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:55</a> | Does formal government censorship still occur in the West? | He says the West has more often used informal censorship, especially private industry cooperating with the state. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=974" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:14</a> | Does 1 Kings 9:26 suggest that yam suf there is really a reed sea rather than the Gulf of Aqaba proper? | Yes; he says the marshy northern setting fits that wording. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=1303" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:43</a> | How should Ezra 4:4-7:23-24 be understood: is Artaxerxes Cambyses II, or did Artaxerxes stop the wall work he had authorized? | He leans toward the latter reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=1583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:23</a> | What are your thoughts on the identity of Darius the Mede? | He treats the text as presenting a real ruler over the empire and works through the passage as a historical problem rather than dismissing it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=2041" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:01</a> | What are your thoughts on the ISV translation? | He has only sampled parts of it and does not want to give a sweeping review without living with it longer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=2281" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:01</a> | If Nabonidus was Belshazzar's father, why does Daniel refer to Nebuchadnezzar as Belshazzar's father? | He says "father" can be broad in biblical Hebrew, and Nabonidus may have had a family tie to Nebuchadnezzar by marriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=2420" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:20</a> | What book or books should a total beginner use to start learning Hebrew? | He recommends an introductory grammar, especially Paige Kelly's textbook. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=2761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:01</a> | Why did Moses's mother place him in the river basket? | He says she hoped an Egyptian would pity and adopt him, not that she knew Pharaoh's daughter would appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=3092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:32</a> | When was the Bible written? | He calls it an anthology written over a long span, with the individual books far earlier than the first complete bound Bibles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=3587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:47</a> | Is the claim accurate that Codex Sinaiticus was written by or on behalf of Origen? | He says Sinaiticus is contemporary with Origen, but that authorship or sponsorship claim is hard to prove. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=3739" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:19</a> | What are some good lay resources for learning ancient Mesopotamian and Egyptian history? | He points to Oxford reference works and especially Ian Shaw for Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=4328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:08</a> | What are your thoughts on the Jerusalem Step Street or Pilgrim Road underground excavation? | He does not have strong settled views but regards the excavation itself as interesting and worthwhile. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=4736" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:56</a> | Who was president of Egypt when the Qurna evictions happened? | He says Egypt still had a monarchy then; the evictions began around the 1940s before Nasser. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=4979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:59</a> | What is going on in Exodus 33:18-22 when God shows Moses his grace? | He treats the passage as centering on God's glory and God's sovereign choice to show grace. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=5358" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:18</a> | Could you give a brief outline of how some of the ten plagues played out in reality? | He sketches them as intensified natural flood and weather events used by God in extraordinary ways. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=5571" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:51</a> | Have you ever been interested in Warhammer 40k, and do you like it? | No; he says he never got into it, even though he had friends who did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=5741" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:41</a> | Are any Egyptian dances known today that likely go back to antiquity, and does their music survive? | He says no clear continuity survives for either the dances or the music. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=6072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:12</a> | Does 2 Samuel 24:1 and 1 Chronicles 21:1 show progressive revelation? | He says they are the same event viewed through different lenses, not really progressive revelation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=6510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:30</a> | Have you read Garrett's commentary on Exodus 4 and the bridegroom of blood episode? | No; he says he has not read Garrett on that passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=6884" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:44</a> | What is your view of sacrificing the red heifer in preparation for a third temple? | He says it would be necessary if temple service were actually going to resume. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=6953" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:53</a> | Is it true that only eight adequately qualified red heifers have ever been found or used? | He says the tradition is that only eight were used, but probably more have been found. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:16</a> | How do you tell which commands are for Christians generally and which were specifically for Israel? | He points to covenant context, with temple and ritual laws tied to Israel while moral commands are reiterated more broadly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7205" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:05</a> | Is there any good reason to date Daniel to the second century? | He says the usual case depends too much on anti-prophecy assumptions and does not fit the linguistic evidence well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:27</a> | Why do critics hate the book of Daniel? | He says Daniel draws hostility because its prophecies look fulfilled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:08</a> | If you could find one original Bible manuscript, which one would you choose? | He would choose Exodus because it would resolve major textual and linguistic questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:34</a> | Are there any ancient documents for which we do have autographs? | Yes; he says some less-copied documents such as treaties survive in autograph form. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=7886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:26</a> | Can the clan view date the flood to the Mesopotamian Early Dynastic period archaeologically? | No; he says honorary transposition and archaeology prevent that kind of precise flood dating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=8100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:00</a> | Were Israel's religious practices different in the Late Bronze Age than in the first century, and is there a resource on that? | Yes; he points especially to changes in temple and tabernacle practice over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=8393" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:53</a> | How would people cast lots in ancient Israel? | He describes lots as dice-like objects, including Urim and Thummim-style lots and throwing-stick lots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=8730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:30</a> | Was the Masoretic Text altered to make prophecies about Jesus less obvious? | He rejects that as a conspiracy theory and prefers ordinary textual transmission problems as the explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=8895" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:15</a> | What does Joseph's Egyptian name mean? | He tentatively parses it as something like "he is said to be the living one." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:42</a> | If God sustains creation but is contained in his temple, does sacred space theology result in two gods? | No; he explains it through an essence-energies distinction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:32</a> | Is the essence-energies distinction the same as Eastern Orthodox teaching, or is it found in Protestant theology too? | He says it is developed especially in Eastern Orthodoxy, though Western churches can still draw on it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:40</a> | Were fly and lice invasions common in ancient Egypt? | Yes; he says both were persistent problems, with lice attested as far back as predynastic contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9551" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:11</a> | What is the difference between Eastern Orthodox energy ideas and New Thought? | He says New Thought treats energy as manipulable by people, while Orthodox energy remains under God's sovereign control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9608" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:08</a> | Is the essence-energies distinction a contradiction of divine simplicity? | No; he says it does not contradict divine simplicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:10</a> | Does the essence-energies distinction apply to God binding himself to the sacraments without being bound by them? | He is not sure and says he has not yet received a good answer from Eastern Orthodox interlocutors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:01</a> | If God is non-physical, can his essence still be in the temple without becoming spatial? | He argues spiritual realities can still have place-like or spatial properties without becoming physical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:47</a> | Does Gavin Ortlund's view of Protestantism as a continual renewal movement resonate with you? | Yes; he says Protestantism remains valid when it keeps reforming toward Christian maturity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=9982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:22</a> | What is Dr. Falk's denomination? | He declines to specify and answers only that he is Christian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10016" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:56</a> | What does continual reforming mean? | He describes it as continually reforming toward a mature, properly ordered Christian life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10149" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:09</a> | Are hermits sinning by being hermits? | No, but he says hermits lose the benefits of Christian community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:56</a> | Why should omnipresence be thrown out? | He says full omnipresence creates theological problems by collapsing sacred and profane space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10635" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:15</a> | When people say God is omnipresent, is that a confusion of God himself or just of the terms they use? | He thinks it usually reflects confusion about God rather than merely sloppy wording. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10829" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:29</a> | Are Old Testament texts about God's limited knowledge more literal than anthropomorphic? | He says he has not seen a text like that which could not be otherwise explained, but would need a specific example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=10853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:53</a> | Is omnipresence a child of Greek or Western thought? | No; he says it is more associated with Eastern and pantheistic thought than Greek thought. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:22</a> | How did Eastern thought influence early Christianity and the church fathers? | He says direct influence was limited, even though trade put Eastern ideas in contact with the Mediterranean world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11336" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:56</a> | How credible is a mythicist claim dating the New Testament to the second century by stylometry and anachronisms? | He rejects it, citing early manuscript and patristic evidence and calling stylometry unreliable for that claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11433" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:33</a> | Do we know which specific Jewish group Philo of Alexandria or Crispus belonged to? | He says no precise faction is known in that case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:56</a> | Were Seth-her-khepeshef and Amun-her-khepeshef different sons of Ramesses II? | He thinks they are the same person using different names while testing a royal religious agenda. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11809" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:49</a> | Did Pharisees or Sadducees exist in Nero's court? | He allows that Pharisees are possible, but says Sadducees would not fit that setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:44</a> | If Yahweh resembles descriptions of Israel's neighbors' gods, is that the Lord meeting people in their culture? | He says some similarities are expected, but warns against reducing them to identity through parallelomania. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=11976" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:36</a> | Do we know which form of Judaism Nero's wife Poppaea adhered to? | He says he does not know and treats it as outside his wheelhouse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:03</a> | Were there Gnostic Jews? | Yes; he says Gnosticism can adapt to many religions and existed in Jewish forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12262" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:22</a> | Are Mandaeans remnants of John the Baptist's followers? | No; he identifies them as a later Zoroastrian Gnostic sect, not John the Baptist's followers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12340" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:40</a> | What is the name of the book on the Mandaeans? | He points to work by Kulp and Schenke and notes that the books may be hard to find and may not be translated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12527" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:47</a> | Who was the person on the Mose stela, and can he be linked with Moses? | He says the name is common in Egypt and the person on the stela is not the biblical Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12625" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:25</a> | Do we know what scroll was found in Josiah's day? | He says Deuteronomy is the most likely answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=12947" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:47</a> | Is it normal that the Veloca Rabbit channel link has no content? | No; he says the publisher retracted the music content, so that link is no longer good. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=13209" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:09</a> | Why not self-publish the audio project? | He says self-publishing would not solve their distribution and monetization problems, especially from Canada. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=13518" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:18</a> | Why don't artists just create their own publishers? | He says access and gatekeeping are the problem, not lack of desire. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=13749" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:09</a> | Was your visual novel already released, or is it still coming later? | It is planned for later release. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=14401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:01</a> | Are there any serious options on hell besides annihilationism, eternal conscious torment, and universalism? | Yes; he says there are several additional models beyond that usual three-part list. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/EYAWYyFXlT8?t=14590" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:10</a> | Is annihilationism just ceasing to exist, and therefore more merciful? | He says no; in his description, annihilationism still involves a period of torment before final destruction. |
